metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2345
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1; Shisa7 is a type-I transmembrane protein interacting with AMPA-type receptors
Figure 2; Shisa7 effects on AMPAR desensitization rate and recovery from desensitization
Figure 3; Shisa7 prolongs synaptic AMPAR currents
Figure 6; Deletion of Shisa7 slows down initiation and decreases maintenance of LTP

Both analyses compare Shisa7 KO mice to WT littermates. Experimental description: "All electrophysiological recordings on CA1 neurons, as well as behavioral analyses, were performed on 8-12 week old male littermates. Male Shisa7 KO and WT littermates were single-housed when adult (>8 weeks),~2 weeks prior to behavioral analysis; behavior was carried out with mice 10-12 weeks"

24/01/2018 Pim
- Overexpressed Shisa7 is of mouse species: "The full-length coding DNA for exon4-containing mouse Shisa7 (reference NP_766325.3)"
